body_txt: Many graphical tropes are simplifications, used to make the scene easier to draw. Books are always open in the middle, bite marks are smooth, and so on. It should be noted that, despite the name, these tropes don't necessarily mean that the company/artist using them was just being lazy by doing so. They might not have had the budget to afford artists who could draw/animate a particularly hard or detailed scene. They might have been working under time constraints. They might find that they have not yet gained enough skills to draw a particular thing, but they are a ways into the project already and can't just back out. They might have devoted a lot of their time and budget to a particular moment and are now working with what they have, or the trope's use may even be genuinely stylized or artistic. These tropes often go hand-in-hand with Limited Animation . Prominent examples:
